---
title: "Data Entry and Storage"
date: today
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: default
    slide-number: true
---

## Dataset Critique

![](01_DataEntry_files/image1.png)

## Our goal is [tidy data]{.underline}

![](01_DataEntry_files/image2.jpeg)

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![](01_DataEntry_files/image3.jpeg)

## Make it a rectangle

:::::: columns
::: {.column width="33%"}
![](01_DataEntry_files/image4.png)
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
![](01_DataEntry_files/image5.png)
:::

::: {.column width="33%"}
![](01_DataEntry_files/image6.png)
:::
::::::

## Make it a rectangle

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Untidy:**

![](01_DataEntry_files/image5.png)

![](01_DataEntry_files/image7.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Tidy:**

![](01_DataEntry_files/image8.png)

![](01_DataEntry_files/image9.png)
:::
:::::

## One value per cell

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Untidy:**

![](01_DataEntry_files/image10.png)

![](01_DataEntry_files/image11.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Tidy:**

![](01_DataEntry_files/image12.png)

![](01_DataEntry_files/image13.png)
:::
:::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

![](01_DataEntry_files/image14.png)

## Avoid confusing the computer

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Untidy:**

![](01_DataEntry_files/image15.png)

![](01_DataEntry_files/image16.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Tidy:**

![](01_DataEntry_files/image17.png)

![](01_DataEntry_files/image18.png)
:::
:::::

## Naming things

![](01_DataEntry_files/image19.png)

## Just for fun!

![](01_DataEntry_files/image20.png)

## Be clear and consistent

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Untidy:**

![](01_DataEntry_files/image21.png)

![](01_DataEntry_files/image22.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**Tidy:**

![](01_DataEntry_files/image23.png)
:::
:::::

## Dealing with dates

If you want your dates all in one column, use "Format cells" to ensure dates are entered and returned in the same format.

![](01_DataEntry_files/image24.png)

Alternatively, right-click on the selected data and select "Format Cells"

## One table of each type of data

**Untidy:**

![](01_DataEntry_files/image25.png)

![](01_DataEntry_files/image26.png)

## One table of each type of data

You need at least one unifying column.

**Tidy:**

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
![](01_DataEntry_files/image27.png)
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](01_DataEntry_files/image28.png)

![](01_DataEntry_files/image29.png)
:::
:::::

## One table per sheet...

![](01_DataEntry_files/image30.png)

Human-readable but not computer-readable

## ...and one sheet per file

![](01_DataEntry_files/image31.png)

If the same type of data (observational unit) is in each sheet, collapse it all down into one sheet. Otherwise, save them as separate files instead.

## Data Storage

"Comma separated value"

::::: columns
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Excel (.xlsx)**

-   Proprietary
-   Can have multiple sheets
-   Cannot be edited in text editors
-   Takes more memory
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
**CSV**

-   Non-proprietary
-   Only one sheet
-   Can be edited in text editors
-   More efficient in storage
:::
:::::

![](01_DataEntry_files/image32.jpeg)

## "Save As" a CSV for storage

![](01_DataEntry_files/image33.png)
